content: Implement retry logic for the auth module with exponential backoff.
I'll add a retry wrapper around the authenticate function. The wrapper will use exponential backoff with a maximum of 3 retries and a base delay of 1 second.
Good, but also add a circuit breaker so we don't hammer the auth service when it's down.
Adding a circuit breaker with a failure threshold of 5 and a cooldown period of 30 seconds. When the circuit is open, auth calls will fail fast without retrying.
The circuit breaker is causing a timeout after 30 seconds. Can you investigate?
The issue is that the circuit breaker's cooldown timer isn't being reset when the service recovers. I'm working on a fix that checks service health with a probe request before resetting the circuit.
